name: John Cage
abstract: John Milton Cage Jr. (September 5, 1912 – August 12, 1992) was an American composer, artist, and music theorist.

works_created.t: 18' 12", A Simultaneous Performance of Cage Works
H P S C H D
String Quartet in Four Parts: Quodlibet
Strings Quartet in Four Parts: Nearly Stationary
She is Asleep: 1. Quartet for Percussion

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: That’s why I didn’t hesitate to suggest recording Cage’s music and Hovhaness’s music... The very first recording of Hovhaness and Cage has my signature at the bottom in the Armenian alphabet. Later on I made more recordings with them and helped get many more recordings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I was introduced to people like Harry Partch and John Cage... one of the reasons I got attracted to that was because in school... I was introduced to people like... John Cage
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 17, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Darius Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Jean Jackson who was very close to Xenia Cage, John Cage’s wife, who was a frequent visitor, which meant that sometimes Virgil Thompson came up to the country to hang out
about_quotes.doc_title: Darius Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 29, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Look at the career John Cage made. I have a picture on my desk of him, and he’s laughing like hell, and I’ve written on it, “Why is this man laughing, and at whom?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kidd Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I was over at the library listening at John Cage... So I left from John Cage, early in the evening, to catch Louis Armstrong at the field house.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kidd Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jonathan Scales
about_quotes.evidence: I’m a big fan of John Cage. You know John Cage the thinker of a composer? The philosopher?
about_quotes.doc_title: Jonathan Scales interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 23, 2025
